author: Julian Treasure
name: The H.A.I.L Cornerstones
summary: Four foundations for speech that people actually want to listen to.

author: Mel Robbins
name: The 5-Second Rule
summary: Count 5-4-3-2-1 to interrupt self-doubt and launch yourself into action before anxiety sets in.

author: Amy Cuddy
name: The Pre-Talk Power Pose
summary: Two minutes of expansive posture before a speech measurably increases confidence and reduces cortisol.

author: Vinh Giang
name: Conversational Threading
summary: Never run out of things to say by identifying multiple threads in any topic and following them naturally.
steps: Listen for 3 threads in what they said

author: Vinh Giang
name: Match, Mirror, and Lead
summary: Build instant rapport by mirroring energy and body language before gently leading the tone of the conversation.

author: Team Cialdini
name: Science of Persuasion
summary: Adding a reason to a request increases compliance because listeners process intent faster.
steps: Let the listener fill in approval

author: Vanessa Van Edwards
name: The Hand Trust Rule
summary: Visible, open hands instantly signal safety and boost listener trust before you speak.
steps: Keep hands above the table at all times
Use open, upward-facing palms when making points
Restrict gestures to the 'Power Box' (chin to hips)

author: Vanessa Van Edwards
name: The Engagement Triple-Nod
summary: Three slow, deliberate nods signal understanding and encourage the speaker to elaborate.

author: Vanessa Van Edwards
name: The Eye-Contact Triangle
summary: Rotate eye contact in a calm triangle pattern to appear warm, composed, and engaged.
steps: Look at the left eye (1-2 sec)
Shift to the right eye (1-2 sec)
Drop to the mouth briefly (1 sec)

author: Vinh Giang
name: The 3-2-1 Trick
summary: Stop rambling by forcing your brain to speak in a clear, numbered structure.

author: Vinh Giang
name: The P.R.E.P Framework
summary: A gold standard for clear, structured opinions that project authority.

author: Vinh Giang
name: The P.A.R.A Method
summary: Create depth without length by turning facts into crisp micro-stories.

author: Chris Anderson
name: The Throughline
summary: Connect every part of your speech to one central idea people remember.
steps: Connect every story to that idea

author: Donald Miller
name: StoryBrand
summary: Position your audience as the hero and yourself as the guide for clear, persuasive messaging.
